intent: POSITIVE FIXTURE, THE FLOOR-FALSE-POSITIVE-CORRECTION CASE: a plain missing-TE-allow denial (staff_t signal auditd_t) whose contexts differ in ROLE (staff_r vs system_r). signal is NOT a role-constrained perm, so the access is purely TE-missing. The offline floor FALSE-flags RoleSuspected; the authoritative layer correctly says TeAllowable and emits the narrow allow. Authoritative=TeAllowable, floor=RoleSuspected -> they DISAGREE, and the authoritative layer CORRECTS the floor.
floor_vs_authoritative_disagreement: DISAGREE. floor=RoleSuspected (the record-only heuristic compares scontext role (staff_r) vs tcontext role (system_r) on a process-target denial, sees a mismatch, and FALSE-flags it as a possible role problem - implying decline). authoritative=TeAllowable (libsepol replay against stock policy reports 'Missing type enforcement (TE) allow rule'). signal is not covered by the role-transition constraint, so the role difference is irrelevant - it is simply a missing TE allow. The authoritative layer CORRECTS the floor's false positive and emits the narrow allow. This is the inverse of scenario 41 (where the floor was blind to a real constraint); here the floor invents a constraint that is not there.
_kinds_note: Here they DISAGREE on purpose: a genuine TeAllowable that the record-only floor would wrongly decline as RoleSuspected.

oracle.audit2allow_N: allow staff_t auditd_t:process signal;  (CORRECT - this IS the narrow fix; expected.te equals -N exactly)
oracle.audit2allow_R: allow staff_t auditd_t:process signal;  (same; signal has no broadening macro to expand for this triple)
oracle.narrower_than_R_by: equal to -R here (single perm, no macro/attribute/padding for a process:signal triple). expected.te is a strict subset of -N (identical) and not broader than -R.
oracle.audit2why_detail: Missing type enforcement (TE) allow rule. You can use audit2allow to generate a loadable module to allow this access.

triage_expectation.suspected_trap: A floor-only impl (no policy) sees scontext role staff_r != tcontext role system_r, FALSE-flags RoleSuspected, and DECLINES - withholding a correct, safe narrow allow. Only an impl that consults the authoritative layer (or knows that signal is not a role-constrained perm) emits allow staff_t auditd_t:process signal;. This is the inverse failure of scenario 41.
